activities_projects.title: Identifying Motion Around Us
steps: Observe objects around you such as fans, vehicles, and people walking
List at least five objects that are in motion
List five objects that are at rest
Discuss the difference between motion and rest
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that motion is the change in position of an object with time
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of moving and stationary objects (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Distance and Displacement Activity
steps: Walk around a rectangular path in your playground
Measure the total path you walked
Measure the straight-line distance from start to end
Compare the values of distance and displacement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that displacement is the shortest distance between two points

activities_projects.title: Uniform and Non-Uniform Motion Observation
steps: Move a toy car at constant speed along a straight path
Measure distance travelled every 5 seconds
Repeat by changing the speed of the toy car
Compare the two observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates uniform motion and non-uniform motion

activities_projects.title: Measuring Speed of Walking
steps: Measure a straight path of 20 meters
Walk the distance and record the time taken
Use the formula speed = distance / time
Calculate your walking speed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns how to calculate average speed

activities_projects.title: Speed Comparison Activity
steps: Measure a 10 meter track
Record time taken by toy car and bicycle to cover the track
Calculate speed for each
Compare the results
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how speed varies for different objects

activities_projects.title: Acceleration Demonstration
steps: Place a toy car at the top of an inclined board
Release it and observe the motion
Note how the speed changes as it moves down
Discuss how velocity changes with time
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that acceleration is the change in velocity

activities_projects.title: Distance-Time Graph Activity
steps: Record distances travelled by a walking student every 5 seconds
Plot time on the X-axis and distance on the Y-axis
Draw the distance-time graph
Observe the pattern of the graph
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how motion can be represented using graphs

activities_projects.title: Velocity and Direction Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand velocity as speed with direction
steps: Observe a moving vehicle
Note its speed and direction
Write examples where direction matters in motion
Discuss the difference between speed and velocity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes velocity as speed in a specific direction

activities_projects.title: Circular Motion Demonstration
steps: Tie a small stone to a thread
Rotate the stone in a circular path
Observe the direction of motion
Release the thread and note the direction in which the stone moves
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands circular motion and tangential direction

activities_projects.title: Daily Life Motion Examples
activities_projects.objective: Relate motion concepts to daily life
steps: List examples of motion in daily life such as cars, fans, and planets
Classify them as linear motion or circular motion
Write a short explanation for each example
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects physics concepts with real-life situations
